```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && for f in content/english/notes/*.md; do stem=$(basename "$f" .md); if [ "$stem" = "_index" ] || [ "$stem" = "getting-started" ] || [ "$stem" = "maps-of-content" ] || [ "$stem" = "mental-models-list" ] || [ "$stem" = "graph" ]; then continue; fi; if grep -q '^## ' "$f" && ! grep -q '^## See also' "$f" || grep -c '^## ' "$f" | grep -qv '^1$'; then count=$(grep -c '^## ' "$f" 2>/dev/null || echo 0); if [ "$count" -gt 1 ]; then echo "$stem: $count sections"; fi; fi; done
```

<summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com &amp;&amp; python3 -c &#34;
from pathlib import Path
import re
NOTES = Path(&#39;content/english/notes&#39;)
SKIP = {&#39;_index&#39;, &#39;graph&#39;, &#39;maps-of-content&#39;, &#39;getting-started&#39;, &#39;mental-models-list&#39;}
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob(&#39;*.md&#39;)):
    if p.stem in SKIP: continue
    text = p.read_text()
    body = text.split(&#39;---&#39;, 2)[-1] if text.startswith(&#39;---&#39;) else text
    idx = body.find(&#39;## See also&#39;)
    main = body[:idx] if idx &gt;= 0 else body
    words = len(main.split())
    has_status = &#39;status: evergreen&#39; in text
    has_formula = bool(re.search(r&#39;\*\*[^*]+\*\*\s*=&#39;, main))
    issues = []
    if not has_status: issues.append(&#39;no-evergreen&#39;)
    if not has_formula: issues.append(&#39;no-formula&#39;)
    if words &gt; 120: issues.append(f&#39;long({words}w)&#39;)
    if issues:
        print(f&#39;{p.stem}: {\&#34;, \&#34;.join(issues)}&#39;)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
import re
NOTES = Path('content/english/notes')
SKIP = {'_index', 'graph', 'maps-of-content', 'getting-started', 'mental-models-list'}
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob('*.md')):
    if p.stem in SKIP: continue
    text = p.read_text()
    body = text.split('---', 2)[-1] if text.startswith('---') else text
    idx = body.find('## See also')
    main = body[:idx] if idx >= 0 else body
    words = len(main.split())
    has_status = 'status: evergreen' in text
    has_formula = bool(re.search(r'\*\*[^*]+\*\*\s*=', main))
    issues = []
    if not has_status: issues.append('no-evergreen')
    if not has_formula: issues.append('no-formula')
    if words > 120: issues.append(f'long({words}w)')
    if issues:
        print(f'{p.stem}: {\", \".join(issues)}')
"
```

from pathlib import Path
NOTES = Path(&#39;content/english/notes&#39;)
SKIP = {&#39;_index&#39;, &#39;graph&#39;, &#39;maps-of-content&#39;, &#39;getting-started&#39;, &#39;mental-models-list&#39;}
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob(&#39;*.md&#39;)):
    if p.stem in SKIP: continue
    text = p.read_text()
    body = text.split(&#39;---&#39;, 2)[-1] if text.startswith(&#39;---&#39;) else text

    main = body[:idx].strip() if idx &gt;= 0 else body.strip()
    paras = [x.strip() for x in main.split(&#39;\n\n&#39;) if x.strip()]
    if len(paras) &gt; 2:
        print(f&#39;{p.stem}: {len(paras)} paras&#39;)
    elif len(paras) == 2 and len(paras[1].split()) &gt; 80:
        print(f&#39;{p.stem}: long para2 ({len(paras[1].split())}w)&#39;)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
NOTES = Path('content/english/notes')
SKIP = {'_index', 'graph', 'maps-of-content', 'getting-started', 'mental-models-list'}
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob('*.md')):
    if p.stem in SKIP: continue
    text = p.read_text()
    body = text.split('---', 2)[-1] if text.startswith('---') else text
    idx = body.find('## See also')
    main = body[:idx].strip() if idx >= 0 else body.strip()
    paras = [x.strip() for x in main.split('\n\n') if x.strip()]
    if len(paras) > 2:
        print(f'{p.stem}: {len(paras)} paras')
    elif len(paras) == 2 and len(paras[1].split()) > 80:
        print(f'{p.stem}: long para2 ({len(paras[1].split())}w)')
"
```

```
there-is-no-perfect-solution: long para2 (87w)

```

from pathlib import Path
import re
NOTES = Path(&#39;content/english/notes&#39;)
SKIP = {&#39;_index&#39;, &#39;graph&#39;}
missing_status = []
missing_formula = []
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob(&#39;*.md&#39;)):
    if p.stem in SKIP: continue
    text = p.read_text()
    body = text.split(&#39;---&#39;, 2)[-1] if text.startswith(&#39;---&#39;) else text
    idx = body.find(&#39;## See also&#39;)
    main = body[:idx] if idx &gt;= 0 else body
    if &#39;status: evergreen&#39; not in text:
        missing_status.append(p.stem)
    if not re.search(r&#39;\*\*[^*]+\*\*\s*=&#39;, main):
        missing_formula.append(p.stem)
print(&#39;missing status:&#39;, len(missing_status), missing_status[:5])
print(&#39;missing formula:&#39;, len(missing_formula), missing_formula)
&#34;</summary>
```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
import re
NOTES = Path('content/english/notes')
SKIP = {'_index', 'graph'}
missing_status = []
missing_formula = []
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob('*.md')):
    if p.stem in SKIP: continue
    text = p.read_text()
    body = text.split('---', 2)[-1] if text.startswith('---') else text
    idx = body.find('## See also')
    main = body[:idx] if idx >= 0 else body
    if 'status: evergreen' not in text:
        missing_status.append(p.stem)
    if not re.search(r'\*\*[^*]+\*\*\s*=', main):
        missing_formula.append(p.stem)
print('missing status:', len(missing_status), missing_status[:5])
print('missing formula:', len(missing_formula), missing_formula)
"
```

```
missing formula: 0 []

```

```bash
cd /home/jorap/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 -c "
from pathlib import Path
import re
NOTES = Path('content/english/notes')
SKIP = {'_index', 'graph'}
issues = []
for p in sorted(NOTES.glob('*.md')):
    if p.stem in SKIP: continue
    text = p.read_text()
    if text.count('---') > 2:
        issues.append(f'{p.stem}: {text.count(\"---\")} delimiters')
    if 'status: evergreen' not in text:
        issues.append(f'{p.stem}: no status')
print('issues:', issues or 'none')
"
```

_**Agent (default)**_

All notes in the garden are now smart and distilled.

### What changed

**64 notes marked `status: evergreen`** — they were already distilled in content but defaulted to "Seedling" in the UI because `status` wasn't set. They now show as Evergreen on `/notes/` and in status badges.

**7 notes normalized to the `**Title** = definition` opener:**

| Note | New opener |
|------|------------|
| Advantages of Digital Gardens | `**Digital gardens** = public notes that grow linked and imperfect...` |
| Anti-Fragile Systems | `**Anti-fragile systems** = note stacks that survive app death...` |
| Associative Linking | `**Associative linking** = connections by meaning, not folder...` |
| Evergreen vs Fleeting Notes | `**Evergreen vs fleeting notes** = two lanes - sparks vs truth...` |
| GTD vs PARA | `**GTD vs PARA** = tasks in the task app, notes in PARA buckets...` |
| Atomic Notes | `**Atomic notes** = one claim you'd say out loud...` |
| Maps of Content | `**Maps of Content** = hand-written hub notes that index related ideas...` |

**Unchanged (by design):** `getting-started`, `mental-models-list`, and `maps-of-content` keep their section structure as hub/meta pages. `_index` and `graph` stay as utility pages without lifecycle status.

Every atomic note now follows the same pattern: a one-breath definition, a short second paragraph with wikilinks, and Evergreen status. Example:

```17:19:content/english/notes/capture.md
**Capture** = saving what resonates into one inbox you actually trust.

Friction kills capture. One inbox, phone to desktop, weekly process - same as [[The Trusted Inbox]]. If I wouldn't act on it or cite it later, it doesn't get saved. Resonance is the filter, not FOMO. Pairs with [[Building a Second Brain]] and [[Active Knowledge Curation]].
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Recent notes use a simple link card layout instead of the full note-card partial.

To make recent note cards uniform height, the list items need `height: 100%` and the links need `display: flex` with `flex-direction: column` so they fill their grid cells.

The stale badge sits outside the card link, making some cards taller than others. Moving it inside the card or reserving space for it will keep heights uniform.

Title length variation could still break uniformity. A two-line clamp on titles would help. The full note-card grid already handles height correctly, but the recent notes section does not.

The date span should use `mt-auto` to push itself to the bottom of each card. CSS grid naturally stretches items in the same row to match the tallest card, so the main structural fix is ensuring the list items and links fill their grid cells completely.
</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Recent Notes cards now share the same height within each row.

**HTML** (`recent-updated.html`):
- Wrapped each item in `notes-recent-item` so the grid can stretch it
- Moved the stale badge inside the card so it doesn’t add uneven height below the link

**CSS** (`custom.css`):
- Cards use a column flex layout with `height: 100%` so they fill the grid cell
- Titles clamp to 2 lines so long names don’t blow up card height
- Dates sit at the bottom via `margin-top: auto` on the meta row

On the 2-column grid, cards in the same row now match height regardless of title length or stale status.
